key_insight: A 16-bead quasi-2D colloidal cluster under uniaxial tension shows elastic, plastic, and soft-mode deformation, and a simplified spring-mass model captures its fracture behavior near 10% strain.
engineering_relevance: The study provides a minimal mechanics model for predicting how small bonded particulate assemblies deform and fail under tensile loading.
method: Clamp a ribbon-shaped colloidal cluster with optical tweezers and stretch it along the long axis.
Track particle motion by video microscopy to extract stress, strain, and configuration evolution.
Compare experiments with canonical Monte Carlo simulations and a reduced 7-DOF theoretical model.
evidence_signal: Experimental stress-strain curves and configuration distributions were reproduced well by both the full 32-DOF simulations and the simplified 7-DOF model, especially in elastic and soft-mode regimes.
watch_item: The simplified model shows minor discrepancies in the plastic regime, so quantitative use there may require additional calibration.
paper_title: Mechanical response of quasi-two-dimensional colloidal clusters under uniaxial tension
